subject: Biology
title: CRISPR and Immune Engineering
description: A quick, practical guide to CRISPR and Immune Engineering.

hook.text: CRISPR lets scientists edit genes with surgical precision—including genes in immune cells.

buildup.text: Researchers are using CRISPR to enhance T cells, making them better at finding and killing tumors.

discovery.text: CRISPR can also knock out the gene HIV uses to enter T cells—potentially creating HIV-resistant immunity.

twist.text: In 2018, a scientist claimed to have CRISPR-edited human embryos for HIV resistance—sparking global outrage.

climax.text: Gene editing could cure genetic immune disorders, but ethical boundaries remain fiercely debated.

punchline.text: Edit the code. Rewrite the immune system. Carefully.

quiz.question: How could CRISPR potentially combat HIV?
options: By knocking out the gene HIV uses to enter T cells
By creating a vaccine from HIV RNA
By strengthening stomach acid against the virus
